title: Egg White Veggie Omelette

subs.name: 2 whole eggs instead of 4 whites — richer, with healthy fats, around 160 kcal
mushroom, tomato or corn — same cook time

ingredients.name: baby spinach or palak, roughly chopped

subs.name: fenugreek leaves (methi) for a more Indian flavour

steps.text: Crack the eggs and carefully separate the whites from the yolks — any yolk in the whites will prevent them from whipping properly. Whisk the whites with salt and black pepper until slightly frothy and loosened, about 30 seconds.
Dice the capsicum and onion finely; roughly chop the spinach. Mince the green chilli if using. Having everything cut before the pan gets hot keeps the cook fast and controlled.

Heat oil in a non-stick frying pan on medium. Add onion and capsicum and sauté for 2 minutes until softened. Toss in the spinach and green chilli, stir for 30 seconds until wilted, then push the vegetables to the edges of the pan.

Pour the egg whites into the centre of the pan and tilt so they spread evenly underneath the vegetables. Cook undisturbed on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until the edges are fully set and the centre is no longer liquid.
Fold the omelette in half with a spatula and cook 30 seconds more for a firmer set, or slide it open-faced onto a plate. Eat immediately while puffed — egg white omelettes deflate quickly.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → 2 whole eggs instead of 4 whites — richer, with healthy fats, around 160 kcal (higher fat but still high-protein); capsicum → mushroom, tomato or corn — same cook time; spinach → fenugreek leaves (methi) for a more Indian flavour
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Cook on a tawa (flat griddle) for a slightly smoky edge that a non-stick pan cannot match. Add a pinch of haldi (turmeric) and a few curry leaves to the tempering before adding the vegetables for a fully desi makeover. The technique mirrors making a thin besan chilla — same pan, same motion.
